facts.report_excerpt: = Paying Report :description: Current owner-oriented maturity report for the Paying component. :page-slug: paying-report This page is the current owner-oriented maturity report for *Paying*. It is written not as a defect log, but as a public-facing readiness portrait: what the component already demonstrates, what maturity band it currently occupies, and which leader-class direction it is converging toward. == Component Portrait Paying is not shaped as a simple gateway SDK wrapper or a narrow checkout callback handler. It reads as a Symfony-oriented payment lifecycle and payment-operations component: a system intended to manage create, start, finalize, refund, and read flows, canonical API/UI/CLI surfaces, webhook ingest and verification, idempotent operational handling, outbox publishing, health and metrics exposure, provider routing, and evidence-friendly runtime proofing. The component already shows signs of being treated as a governed subsystem rather than as a convenience utility. Its current shape suggests an internal payment and payment-operations platform layer designed for durability, structured evolution, and integration into a wider ecosystem. == Ecosystem Role Within the broader Smartresponsor direction, Paying appears to occupy the role of a payment lifecycle, webhook, and payment-operations component. Its ecosystem role can be understood across several functions: * as a payment create/start/finalize/refund/read surface, * as a provider-routing and provider-asymmetry management layer, * as a webhook ingest and verification surface, * as an idempotent and outbox-aware runtime, * as an operator-console and API documentation surface, * and as a reusable internal payment capability for broader applications. This makes Paying strategically more important than a typical payment provider adapter. It points toward a reusable internal payment capability that other components can depend on. == Current Maturity Paying already presents several strong signals of maturity: * a clear payment-lifecycle product identity, * explicit canonical API, UI, and CLI surfaces, * payment lifecycle orchestration for create/start/finalize/refund/read flows, * webhook ingest, idempotency, outbox publishing, and health/metrics endpoints, * real provider support and verification truth documented in the README, * payment entity lifecycle methods for processing, completed, failed, and refunded states, * unit, functional, e2e, Panther, and Playwright coverage posture, * install/runtime preflight, fixture dry-run, container/doctrine/runtime smokes, * route inventory and runtime proof reports, * and a strong security shell around composer audit, importmap audit, gitleaks, semgrep, and security tests. In its current public reading, the component is no longer in a prototype-only band. It already behaves like a structured component moving toward release-candidate maturity. == RC Scorecard [cols="2,1,4",options="header"] |=== | Layer | Score to RC | Reading | Product identity | 8.9 / 10 | The component is already clearly framed around payment lifecycle and payment operations rather than a generic gateway helper. | Architecture and domain shape | 8.7 / 10 | Workflow, Messenger, rate limiting, provider routing, canonical surfaces, and dual data/infra storage already form a governed payment-runtime architecture. | Runtime and application surface | 8.9 / 10 | Canonical API/UI/CLI, operator console, OpenAPI lint, health, metrics, and demo/e2e surfaces establish a very real application runtime. | QA maturity | 9.0 / 10 | Unit, functional, e2e, Panther, Playwright, fixture bootstrap, doctrine/container/runtime smokes, and proof-pack tooling create a very strong verification contour. | Governance and operational readiness | 9.1 / 10 | Runtime preflight, local pipeline tiers, route inventory, runtime proof, and proof-pack scripting form one of the strongest operational-governance surfaces among the components. | Security posture | 9.2 / 10 | Composer audit, importmap audit, gitleaks, semgrep, webhook verification, idempotency, and security tests make this one of the strongest security contours in the ecosystem. | Payment operations posture | 9.0 / 10 | Lifecycle orchestration, refunds, provider routing, webhooks, idempotency, outbox, status, and metrics together place Paying firmly in a serious payment-operations class. | Market-facing alignment | 8.8 / 10 | The component already aligns well with enterprise payment operations and payment-lifecycle platform classes, though it still reads more as a governed platform layer than a finished public payment suite. | Overall readiness to RC | 9.0 / 10 | Paying is already a very strong pre-RC component with clear enterprise-class internal payment and payment-operations signals. |=== == Leader Line Paying should not be measured against simple provider SDK bindings or lightweight payment-form backends. Its natural comparison line is closer to the enterprise-class payment lifecycle, webhook-driven …

key_file_excerpts.composer.json:
{
    "name": "paying/payment",
    "type": "project",
    "license": "proprietary",
    "require": {
        "php": "^8.4",
        "ext-json": "*",
        "ext-openssl": "*",
        "doctrine/doctrine-bundle": "^2.10",
        "doctrine/doctrine-migrations-bundle": "^3.6",
        "doctrine/orm": "^2.16",
        "nelmio/api-doc-bundle": "^5.1",
        "symfony/asset": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/config": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/console": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/dependency-injection": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/form": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/framework-bundle": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/http-foundation": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/intl": "^8.0",
        "symfony/messenger": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/monolog-bundle": "^3.10",
        "symfony/rate-limiter": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/routing": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/runtime": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/security-bundle": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/serializer": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/twig-bundle": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/uid": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/validator": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/workflow": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/yaml": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "twig/twig": "^3.0"
    },
    "require-dev": {
        "phpunit/phpunit": "^10.5",
        "rector/rector": "^1.2",
        "phpstan/phpstan": "^1.11",
        "doctrine/doctrine-fixtures-bundle": "^3.6",
        "friendsofphp/php-cs-fixer": "^3.66",
        "symfony/browser-kit": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/css-selector": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/dotenv": "^7.1 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/panther": "^2.2",
        "ext-pdo": "*"
    },
    "autoload": {
        "psr-4": {
            "App\\": "src/"
        }
    },
    "config": {
        "platform": {
            "php": "8.4.0"
        },
        "sort-packages": true,
        "process-timeout": 900,
        "allow-plugins": {
            "symfony/runtime": true
        }
    },
    "scripts": {
        "lint": "@php tools/php/php84.php tools/qa/PayingPhpLint.php",
        "server:start": "@php tools/runtime/payment_server_start.php",
        "server:stop": "@php tools/runtime/payment_server_stop.php",
        "cs:fix": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.php",
        "cs:check": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.php --dry-run --diff",
        "cs:check-quiet": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.php --dry-run",
        "stan": "@stan:runtime-target",
        "stan:conservative": "@php tools/php/php82.php vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --memory-limit=1G --debug --no-progress",
        "stan:runtime-target": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --memory-limit=1G --debug --no-progress",
        "test": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration phpunit.xml.dist",
        "test:unit": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration phpunit.xml.dist --testsuite unit",
        "test:functional": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration phpunit.xml.dist --testsuite functional",
        "test:e2e": "bash tools/runtime/payment_panther_e2e.sh",
        "test:ui:playwright": "npm run test:playwright",
        "test:functional:debug": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration phpunit.xml.dist --testsuite functional --stop-on-error --debug",
        "test:debug:first-error": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpunit --configuration phpunit.xml.dist --stop-on-error --debug",
        "lint:yaml": "@php tools/ci/console-test-env.php lint:yaml config --parse-tags",
        "lint:container": "@php tools/ci/console-test-env.php lint:container",
        "docs:openapi:lint": "@php tools/php/php84.php tools/inspection/PaymentOpenApiLint.php",
        "docs:phpdoc": "@php tools/php/php84.php tools/runtime/phpDocumentor.phar -c phpdoc.dist.xml",

verdict.summary: Paying reads as a Symfony-oriented payment lifecycle and operations component with strong documentation, multi-tier testing, and mature local ops tooling; primary gap is visible CI/CD automation in-repo.
strengths: Clear payment lifecycle identity and scope documented
Rich docs set (architecture, API, install, operations, limits)
Multi-tier tests present (unit, functional, e2e, Playwright) with composer/npm scripts
Symfony 7/8 stack with Doctrine ORM, Workflow, Messenger, Security
Static analysis and code quality tooling (phpstan, rector, php-cs-fixer)
Security posture signals (Symfony security-bundle, gitleaks config)
risks: No GitHub Actions or CI workflows detected
Runtime/tooling target PHP 8.4 while environments may vary; ensure alignment
Antora nav/index not detected; report may be orphaned in docs site
Dual Symfony major targets increase test surface and maintenance load
next_actions: Add CI workflows to run phpstan, cs checks, unit/functional/e2e, and Playwright on PRs
Add CI step to run gitleaks and composer audit, and publish artifacts/reports
Introduce release tagging and changelog automation; publish RC builds
Validate and enforce PHP 8.4 runtime in CI containers; document matrix if needed
Wire the Antora report into site navigation so it’s discoverable
Optionally publish container images from compose/Docker tooling for consistent runtime
notes: Assessment grounded on repo tree, composer/npm scripts, and the component’s embedded report
Branch master at 262dc205f1237c2578ed6fb1f576d740e827d273 with no exact tag
Tooling directories and docs indicate strong local governance despite missing hosted CI
Scores retain pre-RC contour with a small ops/overall adjustment for CI visibility

product.summary: The component’s product identity as an internal payment lifecycle and operations platform is clearly articulated and well-documented.
risks: Reads primarily as an internal platform layer; external productization and packaging signals are limited
improvements: Tighten public-facing positioning and usage narratives in README and docs/API.md for non-internal consumers

architecture.summary: Symfony-first architecture with Doctrine ORM, Workflow, Messenger, and clear interfaces suggests a governed, modular domain shape.
risks: Supporting Symfony ^7.1 || ^8.0 increases compatibility and test matrix complexity
improvements: Document key bounded contexts and interface contracts in docs/architecture to aid contributor onboarding

runtime.summary: A real application surface is present (public/index.php, config, compose) with operational docs and scripts indicating runnable environments.
risks: Declared PHP 8.4 platform may diverge from some environments; ensure runtime parity in automation
improvements: Provide a documented, reproducible container image and compose flow used in CI and local runs

qa.summary: Multi-tier testing (unit, functional, e2e, Playwright) and static analysis are wired via composer/npm with strong local QA posture.
risks: Lack of hosted CI may limit consistent execution of the full test matrix on PRs
improvements: Introduce CI pipelines to run phpunit suites, Panther/Playwright e2e, and phpstan on every change

ops.summary: Operational readiness is signaled by compose, docker deploy assets, ops dashboards/alerts, and governance docs, though hosted CI is absent.
risks: No .github/workflows detected; delivery and release automation likely manual
improvements: Add CI workflows for build/test/scan, and automate release tagging and artifact publication

market.summary: Positioning aligns with an enterprise payment lifecycle/operations capability suitable for internal ecosystem reuse.
risks: Market-facing packaging and external adoption signals are limited in-repo
improvements: Expand docs with integration recipes and provider matrices to clarify fit for external consumers

overall.summary: Pre-RC maturity is evident across docs, architecture, runtime, QA, and ops tooling; the main gap is visible CI/CD automation.
risks: Missing hosted CI and release automation can slow RC hardening and confidence
improvements: Stand up CI/CD with tests, static analysis, security scans, and tagged releases to close the RC gap
